yaml.title: Public Literature Evidence
yaml.subtitle: What published studies say about genes, proteins, and productivity

markdown.markdown: The KG integrates **cause-effect relationships** curated from ~500 publications on genetic perturbation in the context of CHO productivity. This chapter covers how to query that evidence layer. --- ## How Literature Evidence is Structured Publications were scouted from **~45,000 CHO-related papers** through a two-stage filter: 1. **Filter 1** — grouped into three categories: - **(A)** Gene/protein/metabolite target manipulation and cell engineering studies ✓ *included* - **(B)** Bioprocess optimisation - **(C)** Other studies 2. **Filter 2** — Inclusion: productivity insights through target manipulation or omics-driven cell-line engineering. Exclusion: not directly relevant to target manipulation impacting productivity. Result: **~500 publications** describing genetic interventions (overexpression, knockdown, knockout) and their effects on productivity. Each extracted relationship is stored as a **directed edge** with properties: | Property | Description | |---|---| | `cause` | Gene / protein / metabolite manipulated | | `manipulation` | OE (overexpression), KD (knockdown), KO (knockout) | | `effect_entity` | Productivity phenotype affected | | `effect_direction` | Positive (+) or negative (−) | | `cell_line` | e.g., CHO-K1 GS KO | | `target_product` | e.g., IgG1 | | `reactor` | e.g., Fed-batch | | `pmid` | PubMed ID of source publication | | `evidence_sentence` | Source text from the paper | --- ## Key Effect Entities The KG tracks effects across four biological dimensions: **Productivity:** specific_productivity_qp · product_titer · volumetric_productivity · secretion_rate · product_yield · production_stability **Growth:** viable_cell_density_vcd · cell_viability · IVCD · specific_growth_rate · apoptosis_rate · cell_cycle_phase_distribution · cloning_efficiency **Metabolic:** lactate_production/consumption · ammonia_production · glucose_consumption · glutamine_metabolism · redox_balance · metabolic_flux · lipid_metabolism **Quality:** glycosylation_profile · fucosylation · sialylation · product_purity · protein_fragmentation · aggregation_level · host_cell_protein_content --- ## Querying Literature Evidence ### Finding All Genes Linked to Productivity ::: {.query-box} Which genes are linked to specific productivity (qP) in published CHO studies? ::: ::: {.query-box} Show all genes with published evidence for improving product titer in CHO cells. ::: ### Looking Up a Specific Gene ::: {.query-box} What does the literature say about HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) and CHO productivity? ::: ::: {.query-box} Summarise all published evidence for ST14 in CHO cells, including manipulation type and effect. ::: ### Filtering by Phenotype ::: {.query-box} Which genes, when overexpressed, positively affect product titer in CHO? ::: ::: {.query-box} Show genes whose knockout reduces protein fragmentation. ::: ### Filtering by Source Organism ::: {.query-box} Show only CHO-specific literature evidence for HSPA5. ::: ::: {.query-box} Are there human or mouse studies for FOXO1 that might be relevant to CHO productivity? ::: ### Retrieving Supporting Evidence Sentences ::: {.query-box} Show the supporting sentences from publications linking HSPA5 overexpression to increased titre. ::: ### Genes with Experimental Manipulation ::: {.query-box} Which genes have been experimentally overexpressed in CHO cells and shown a positive effect on qP? Show PMID and evidence. ::: --- ## Worked Example: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) Literature HSPA5 encodes the ER chaperone GRP78/BiP. Published manipulation studies support overexpression as a productivity lever: | PMID | Manipulation | Effect | Product | |---|---|---|---| | 32624757 | GRP78 OE | ↑ Titre | mAb | | 24081924 | BiP co-expression | ↑ qP | Recombinant protein | | 1373378 | GRP78 OE | ↑ Productivity | IgG | **Mechanism:** UPR activation expands ER folding capacity → improves secretory efficiency. --- ## Worked Example: ST14 Literature ST14 encodes Matriptase, a serine protease. Knockout studies show improved product quality: | PMID | Manipulation | Effect | |---|---|---| | 29777593 | ST14 KO | ↓ Protein fragmentation | | 29777593 | ST14 KO | ↑ Product purity | **Mechanism:** Matriptase degrades secreted product → knockout eliminates fragmentation and improves purity.
markdown.srcMarkdownNoYaml: ## Overview {#sec-literature} The KG integrates **cause-effect relationships** curated from ~500 publications on genetic perturbation in the context of CHO productivity. This chapter covers how to query that evidence layer. --- ## How Literature Evidence is Structured Publications were scouted from **~45,000 CHO-related papers** through a two-stage filter: 1. **Filter 1** — grouped into three categories: - **(A)** Gene/protein/metabolite target manipulation and cell engineering studies ✓ *included* - **(B)** Bioprocess optimisation - **(C)** Other studies 2. **Filter 2** — Inclusion: productivity insights through target manipulation or omics-driven cell-line engineering. Exclusion: not directly relevant to target manipulation impacting productivity. Result: **~500 publications** describing genetic interventions (overexpression, knockdown, knockout) and their effects on productivity. Each extracted relationship is stored as a **directed edge** with properties: | Property | Description | |---|---| | `cause` | Gene / protein / metabolite manipulated | | `manipulation` | OE (overexpression), KD (knockdown), KO (knockout) | | `effect_entity` | Productivity phenotype affected | | `effect_direction` | Positive (+) or negative (−) | | `cell_line` | e.g., CHO-K1 GS KO | | `target_product` | e.g., IgG1 | | `reactor` | e.g., Fed-batch | | `pmid` | PubMed ID of source publication | | `evidence_sentence` | Source text from the paper | --- ## Key Effect Entities The KG tracks effects across four biological dimensions: **Productivity:** specific_productivity_qp · product_titer · volumetric_productivity · secretion_rate · product_yield · production_stability **Growth:** viable_cell_density_vcd · cell_viability · IVCD · specific_growth_rate · apoptosis_rate · cell_cycle_phase_distribution · cloning_efficiency **Metabolic:** lactate_production/consumption · ammonia_production · glucose_consumption · glutamine_metabolism · redox_balance · metabolic_flux · lipid_metabolism **Quality:** glycosylation_profile · fucosylation · sialylation · product_purity · protein_fragmentation · aggregation_level · host_cell_protein_content --- ## Querying Literature Evidence ### Finding All Genes Linked to Productivity ::: {.query-box} Which genes are linked to specific productivity (qP) in published CHO studies? ::: ::: {.query-box} Show all genes with published evidence for improving product titer in CHO cells. ::: ### Looking Up a Specific Gene ::: {.query-box} What does the literature say about HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) and CHO productivity? ::: ::: {.query-box} Summarise all published evidence for ST14 in CHO cells, including manipulation type and effect. ::: ### Filtering by Phenotype ::: {.query-box} Which genes, when overexpressed, positively affect product titer in CHO? ::: ### Retrieving Supporting Evidence Sentences ::: {.query-box} Show the supporting sentences from publications linking HSPA5 overexpression to increased titre. ::: ### Genes with Experimental Manipulation ::: {.query-box} Which genes have been experimentally overexpressed in CHO cells and shown a positive effect on qP? Show PMID and evidence. ::: --- ## Worked Example: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) Literature HSPA5 encodes the ER chaperone GRP78/BiP. Published manipulation studies support overexpression as a productivity lever: | PMID | Manipulation | Effect | Product | |---|---|---|---| | 32624757 | GRP78 OE | ↑ Titre | mAb | | 24081924 | BiP co-expression | ↑ qP | Recombinant protein | | 1373378 | GRP78 OE | ↑ Productivity | IgG | **Mechanism:** UPR activation expands ER folding capacity → improves secretory efficiency. --- ## Worked Example: ST14 Literature ST14 encodes Matriptase, a serine protease. Knockout studies show improved product quality: | PMID | Manipulation | Effect | |---|---|---| | 29777593 | ST14 KO | ↓ Protein fragmentation | | 29777593 | ST14 KO | ↑ Product purity | **Mechanism:** Matriptase degrades secreted product → knockout eliminates fragmentation and improves purity.
